mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by unemployment rates below 3.5%, signals stronger wage growth and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 3.5 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in 50%+ of cases, or their correlation with SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 295) during a monetary tightening cycle increases bond yields, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 295 and FEDFUNDS > 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production (INDPRO > 105) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO > 105.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS > 150k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 3.6) signals a strong labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through elevated consumer spending.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 3.6 fails to produce positive 20d SPX returns in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST > 1550) indicates strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved sector sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST > 1550.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 2.0) during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 295) raises real interest rates, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.0 and CPIAUCSL > 295.
